content: I just got back from a sabbatical traveling through South America and I'm feeling a bit out of shape. I've been working as a geologist in mining exploration for years, but I haven't been prioritizing my own fitness.
Welcome back! It's great that you took time off to travel and explore. South America is a beautiful continent. What were some of your favorite experiences from your trip?
I loved hiking in Patagonia and trying all the local foods. I've always been a fan of origami and I even tried making some traditional Chilean paper crafts.
That sounds like a wonderful experience! Origami is such a unique hobby. Did you find that traveling and being in nature helped you feel more relaxed and centered?
Definitely. I felt really rejuvenated after my trip. I've been trying to get back into archery, which I used to do in college, and I've also been brewing my own beer at home.
Archery is a great way to challenge yourself and relax at the same time. And home brewing is a fun hobby! What kind of beers do you like to brew?
I love brewing IPAs and stouts. I've been experimenting with different recipes and ingredients. I've also been listening to a lot of podcasts on my commute to work in Denver.
IPAs and stouts are great choices! Podcasts are a great way to pass the time during commutes. What kind of podcasts do you enjoy listening to?
I love listening to science and history podcasts. I find them really fascinating and they help me learn new things.
I've been trying to prioritize my own fitness goals, but it's tough with my work schedule. I've been working on a project in the mountains of Colorado and it's been keeping me busy.

It can be challenging to balance work and fitness, especially when you're working on a project with a tight deadline. Maybe we can explore some ways to fit in exercise and self-care amidst your busy schedule?
That would be great, thanks. I've been thinking about trying stand-up comedy as a way to relieve stress and have fun.

Stand-up comedy is a great way to challenge yourself and have fun! It's wonderful that you're exploring different creative outlets. Maybe you can even try incorporating some of your geology knowledge into your comedy routine?
